criteria: Output includes simulation_run_packet, run_manifest, measurement_results, result_verdicts, run_blockers, owner_followup_needed, downstream_handoff, and boundary_review_note.
Case A is blocked/not executed because transistor_model_q1 and measurement definitions are missing.
Case A includes blockers for both missing model and missing measurement definitions.
Case A does not derive measurement results or pass/fail verdicts from nonexistent measurements.
Case A owner follow-up asks for missing model and measurement definitions.
Case B is synthetic-stub-only, not actual simulator execution.
Case B includes m_gain_db = 21.4 dB from synthetic_stub_log.
Case B links pass verdict to r_gain_min_20db / value_db >= 20.
Case B does not claim owner acceptance and routes to owner review when useful.
Boundary notes preserve upstream read-only behavior, no upstream mutation, no invented models, and no private/raw/secret/runtime absolute paths.
hard_fail_conditions: Marks Case A as failed verification solely because execution was blocked.
Provides Case A pass/fail measurement verdicts without measurement definitions.
Claims Case B used an actual simulator run or waveform evidence.
Marks Case B as owner accepted.
Gives Case B pass without naming measurement and rule.
Invents models, measurements, owner decision refs, upstream mutations, or external evidence.
Includes private, raw, secret, credential, or runtime absolute paths.
overfit_guard: Equivalent field names and ordering pass when semantics are unambiguous and no critical section loses material information versus the gpt-5.5/xhigh candidate.
